meta.title: CompTIA A+ Sprint Prep
meta.subtitle: A focused local study app for Core 1 and Core 2 review when the exam date is close.
meta.exam_code_note: Use this as a high-yield companion and still compare against your official current CompTIA exam objectives.

domains.name: Hardware
domains.focus: PC components, displays, storage, printers, connectors, and upgrade decisions.

domains.name: Networking
domains.focus: Ports, protocols, TCP/IP basics, Wi-Fi, routers, switches, and network troubleshooting.
must_know: Common tools: ping, tracert, nslookup, toner, crimper

domains.name: Mobile Devices
domains.focus: Laptops, smartphones, tablets, accessories, batteries, and synchronization methods.
must_know: Mobile display, battery, and port issues
Screen locks, biometrics, and remote wipe

domains.name: Virtualization & Cloud
domains.focus: VM concepts, cloud service models, shared responsibility, and resource pooling.

domains.name: Troubleshooting
domains.focus: The CompTIA troubleshooting methodology plus common hardware and network symptoms.
must_know: Power, overheating, POST, connectivity, and printer issues
Start simple and verify before escalating

domains.name: Operating Systems
domains.focus: Windows editions, tools, commands, macOS/Linux awareness, filesystems, and boot utilities.

domains.name: Security
domains.focus: Physical security, malware types, authentication, least privilege, and secure SOHO practices.
must_know: MFA, permissions, password policy, device hardening

domains.name: Software Troubleshooting
domains.focus: Boot issues, malware symptoms, BSOD, application failures, and remediation steps.
must_know: Safe Mode, recovery options, and system utilities
Browser issues, service failures, and startup symptoms

domains.name: Operational Procedures
domains.focus: Safety, professionalism, backup awareness, documentation, scripting basics, and change management.
must_know: Ticket notes, escalation, and customer communication
Backups, change approval, and acceptable use awareness

flashcards.back: TCP 443. Encrypted web traffic using TLS.
Name resolution. Converts hostnames to IP addresses.
Dynamically assigns IP configuration to clients.
SSD is faster and durable with no moving parts. HDD is slower but often cheaper per GB.
Mirroring for redundancy. Good fault tolerance, not a backup.
Striping with parity. Needs at least 3 disks.
Short-range wireless used for tap-to-pay and quick pairing.
Cloud model delivering compute, storage, and networking infrastructure.
Platform that creates and runs virtual machines.
Identify the problem before changing anything.
Checks and repairs protected Windows system files.
Windows filesystem with permissions, encryption, compression, and journaling.
Users get only the permissions needed to do their job.
Social engineering attempt to steal credentials or sensitive information.
Boot mode using minimal drivers and services for diagnostics.
Helps prevent electrostatic discharge damage while servicing hardware.

glossary.meaning: Blue Screen of Death, a Windows stop error.
Multi-factor authentication using two or more factor types.
Remote Desktop Protocol, commonly on TCP 3389.
Modern firmware interface replacing legacy BIOS in many systems.

cram_sections.title: Troubleshooting Methodology
items: Establish a theory of probable cause
Test the theory to determine cause
Establish a plan of action and implement the solution
Verify full system functionality and implement preventive measures

cram_sections.title: Wi-Fi Snapshot
items: WPA2/WPA3 are preferred modern security choices

sprint_plan: Spend the first pass hammering weak spots, not what already feels easy.
Alternate between timed quizzes and fast active recall with flashcards.
End each session with a 10-minute cram-sheet pass on ports, commands, and troubleshooting order.
Review explanations for every missed question, even when the right answer looked familiar.
